mnemonics: Distributed Systems are Big and Complex
Distributed Transactions are like a Long Journey
keyPoints: 17.1: Introduction to Distributed Transactions - A distributed transaction is a sequence of operations performed across multiple sites.
17.2: Flat Distributed Transactions - A flat transaction is a single, atomic operation that spans multiple sites.
17.3: Nested Distributed Transactions - A nested transaction is a transaction that can be rolled back to its root, even if the outer transaction is committed.
17.4: Types of Distributed Transactions - There are three types of distributed transactions: atomic, consistent, isolated, and durable (ACID) transactions.
17.5: Distributed Transaction Protocol (DTP) - DTP is a standard protocol for managing distributed transactions, established by the X/Open Company.
17.6: Distributed Transaction Management (DTM) - DTM is a mechanism for managing distributed transactions, including transaction coordination and recovery.
